# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cloud monitoring.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
  - name: my-vm-instance
    type: compute.v1.instance
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
      disks:
        - deviceName: boot-disk
          type: PERSISTENT
          boot: true
          autoDelete: true
          initializeParams:
            sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-10
      networkInterfaces:
        - network: global/networks/default
          accessConfigs:
            - name: External NAT
              type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
      metadata:
        items:
          - key: startup-script
            value: |
              #!/bin/bash
              echo "Startup script running"

  - name: my-cloud-sql-instance
    type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
    properties:
      databaseVersion: MYSQL_5_7
      region: us-central1
      settings:
        tier: db-f1-micro
        ipConfiguration:
          authorizedNetworks:
            - value: 0.0.0.0/0
              name: allow-all
          ipv4Enabled: true

  - name: my-cloud-storage-bucket
    type: storage.v1.bucket
    properties:
      name: my-secure-bucket
      location: US
      storageClass: STANDARD
      versioning:
        enabled: true

  - name: my-load-balancer
    type: compute.v1.globalForwardingRule
    properties:
      name: my-load-balancer
      target: $(ref.my-target-proxy.selfLink)
      portRange: 80
      IPAddress: $(ref.my-global-address.address)

  - name: my-global-address
    type: compute.v1.globalAddress
    properties:
      name: my-global-address

  - name: my-target-proxy
    type: compute.v1.targetHttpProxy
    properties:
      name: my-target-proxy
      urlMap: $(ref.my-url-map.selfLink)

  - name: my-url-map
    type: compute.v1.urlMap
    properties:
      name: my-url-map
      defaultService: $(ref.my-backend-service.selfLink)

  - name: my-backend-service
    type: compute.v1.backendService
    properties:
      name: my-backend-service
      backends:
        - group: $(ref.my-instance-group.selfLink)
      healthChecks:
        - $(ref.my-health-check.selfLink)

  - name: my-instance-group
    type: compute.v1.instanceGroup
    properties:
      name: my-instance-group
      zone: us-central1-a
      instances:
        - $(ref.my-vm-instance.selfLink)

  - name: my-health-check
    type: compute.v1.healthCheck
    properties:
      name: my-health-check
      type: HTTP
      requestPath: /
      checkIntervalSec: 10
      timeoutSec: 5
      healthyThreshold: 2
      unhealthyThreshold: 2

outputs:
  - name: instanceIp
    value: $(ref.my-vm-instance.networkInterfaces[0].accessConfigs[0].natIP)
